ID:       ddx-868ac26c
Title:    Story 16.3: Specs + cross-cutting e2e + raw-transcript-viewed audit even: part 1
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, story:16, ac-quality:needs-refinement, decomposed
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-b4c5151a
Deps:     ddx-5bad54ca, ddx-73749d13
Desc:     PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-b4c5151a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-70a432b5 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-*.md: missing a Run-detail-page subsection describing the trust boundary ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args') and evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-*.md: Run resolver contract does not include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, or normalized ToolCallEntry.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-*.md: review surface does not point to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- No 'raw transcript viewed' audit event exists in the server or schema.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (16.1 page) and ddx-4cd64068 (16.2 evidence tab).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-008: add Run-detail-page subsection with trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- Add 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server (emitted on prompt/response/transcript access).
- Add cross-cutting e2e: full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end via Playwright.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (that is Story 16.1 and 16.2).
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- p95 dataset (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files) all tabs render and become interactive within one render frame.
- bun run test:e2e green.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling

# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    git-config-health:
      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before committing."

    beads-tracker-health:
      run: "sh scripts/beads-tracker-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Beads tracker has unstaged changes. Run 'git add .ddx/beads.jsonl'."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:
    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
    #   glob: "*.go"
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
        else
          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
        fi
      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"

    # Go formatting - native gofmt
    go-fmt:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Format"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
          exit 1
        fi
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
    go-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Lint"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
        for pkg in $packages; do
          golangci-lint run --timeout=60s --new-from-rev=HEAD~1 "$pkg"
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go build - only for changed packages
    go-build:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Build"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: 'go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...'
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
    evidence-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Unbounded prompt sink detected in staged file. Route through internal/evidence or add `// evidence:allow-unbounded reason=\"...\"`."

    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
    runtime-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."

    # Go tests - targeted testing
    go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race -timeout 30m "./$pkg"
          fi
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      env:
        CGO_ENABLED: "1"
        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"

    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
    secrets:

---
{
  "name": "ddx",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "description": "Document-Driven Development eXperience — toolkit for AI-assisted development.",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/easel/ddx.git"
  },
  "license": "ISC",
  "scripts": {
    "design:export": "bunx @google/design.md export --format tailwind .stitch/DESIGN.md > cli/internal/server/frontend/design-tokens.json",
    "test:e2e": "cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/easel/ddx#readme",
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/easel/ddx/issues"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@google/design.md": "0.1.0"
  }
}

cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3359:  """Approve a proposed operator-prompt bead, transitioning it from proposed → open so the execute-loop can claim it. Requires a valid CSRF token. The originating identity must be on the per-project auto-approve allowlist; ts-net identities are never permitted (locked decision). An audit event of kind operator_prompt_approve records the approver. Errors when the bead is missing, is not an operator-prompt, is not in the proposed status, or the caller is not on the allowlist."""
cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3362:  """Cancel a proposed operator-prompt bead, transitioning it from proposed → cancelled so it never enters the execute-loop. Requires a valid CSRF token. Any trusted identity (localhost OR ts-net) may cancel a proposal — the allowlist gates auto-approval, not rejection. An audit event of kind operator_prompt_cancel records the canceller. Errors when the bead is missing, is not an operator-prompt, or is not in the proposed status."""

import { gql } from 'graphql-request'

export const RUN_DETAIL_QUERY = gql`
	query RunDetailExpand($id: ID!) {
		run(id: $id) {
			id
			layer
			status
			projectID
			beadId
			artifactId
			parentRunId
			childRunIds
			startedAt
			completedAt
			durationMs

			queueInputs
			stopCondition
			selectedBeadIds

			baseRevision
			resultRevision
			worktreePath
			mergeOutcome
			checkResults

			harness
			provider
			model
			promptSummary
			powerMin
			powerMax
			tokensIn
			tokensOut
			costUsd
			outputExcerpt
			evidenceLinks
			prompt
			response
			stderr
			billingMode
			outcome
			detail
			cachedTokens
		}
	}
`

export const RUN_EVIDENCE_QUERY = gql`
	query RunEvidenceFiles($id: ID!) {
		run(id: $id) {
			id
			bundleFiles {
				path
				size
				mimeType
			}
		}
	}
`

export const RUN_BUNDLE_FILE_QUERY = gql`
	query RunBundleFileFetch($id: ID!, $path: String!) {
		runBundleFile(id: $id, path: $path) {
			path
			content
			sizeBytes
			truncated
			mimeType
		}
	}
`

export const RUN_SESSION_QUERY = gql`
	query RunSessionExpand($id: ID!) {
		agentSession(id: $id) {
			id
			workerId
			harness
			model
			cost
			billingMode
			baseRev
			resultRev
			stdoutPath
			stderrPath
			tokens {
				prompt
				completion
				total
				cached
			}
			status
			outcome
			prompt
			response
			stderr
		}
	}
`

export const RUN_TOOL_CALLS_QUERY = gql`
	query RunToolCallsExpand($id: ID!, $first: Int, $after: String) {
		runToolCalls(id: $id, first: $first, after: $after) {
			edges {
				node {
					id
					seq
					name: tool
					inputs: input
					output
					error
					durationMs
				}
				cursor
			}
			pageInfo {
				hasNextPage
				endCursor
			}
			totalCount
		}
	}
`

---
export interface RunDetail {
	id: string
	layer: string
	status: string
	projectID: string | null
	beadId: string | null
	artifactId: string | null
	parentRunId: string | null
	childRunIds: string[]
	startedAt: string | null
	completedAt: string | null
	durationMs: number | null

	queueInputs: string | null
	stopCondition: string | null
	selectedBeadIds: string[] | null

	baseRevision: string | null
	resultRevision: string | null
	worktreePath: string | null
	mergeOutcome: string | null
	checkResults: string | null

	harness: string | null
	provider: string | null
	model: string | null
	promptSummary: string | null
	powerMin: number | null
	powerMax: number | null
	tokensIn: number | null
	tokensOut: number | null
	costUsd: number | null
	outputExcerpt: string | null
	evidenceLinks: string[] | null
	prompt: string | null
	response: string | null
	stderr: string | null
	billingMode: string | null
	outcome: string | null
	detail: string | null
	cachedTokens: number | null

	bundleFiles?: BundleFile[]
}

export interface BundleFile {
	path: string
	size: number
	mimeType: string
}

export interface BundleFileContent {
	path: string
	content: string | null
	sizeBytes: number
	truncated: boolean
	mimeType: string
}

export interface ToolCall {
	id: string
	name: string
	seq: number
	inputs: string | null
	output: string | null
	error: string | null
	durationMs: number | null
}

export interface SessionDetail {
	id: string
	workerId: string | null
	harness: string
	model: string
	cost: number | null
	billingMode: string
	baseRev: string | null
	resultRev: string | null
	stdoutPath: string | null
	stderrPath: string | null
	tokens: { prompt: number | null; completion: number | null; total: number | null; cached: number | null } | null
	status: string
	outcome: string | null
	prompt?: string | null
	response?: string | null
	stderr?: string | null
}

data.text: I’m patching in a single focused Playwright test in `runs.spec.ts`. It will mock a 200-call tool trace plus 30 evidence files, walk the tabs, paginate the tool list to the end, and verify one inline evidence view opens, which gives us direct evidence for the p95-scale acceptance without changing the component behavior.
The large-dataset test is in place. I’m running the new run-detail case first to catch any route-mock or selector mistakes before I pay for the whole frontend e2e suite.

data.text: The tools tab behavior is what we expected: it pages in 50-call chunks, so the right assertion is the final 200-row state after the load-more loop, not the initial 50-row window. I’m moving that check to the end of the pagination walk and rerunning the focused spec.

data.text: The evidence inline-view assertion is the only remaining issue. I’m switching it to a direct row-specific selector and a less brittle visibility/content check, since the component renders the inline block asynchronously after the route fetch completes.

data.text: I’ve checked the evidence component itself: the inline block appears only after the row-specific fetch resolves, so I’m making the test target the exact `data-evidence-view` row and asserting the toggle state before checking content. That avoids false negatives from a generic `View` locator.

data.text: The new p95-scale spec passes on its own. I’m running the full frontend `test:e2e` gate now, since that’s the remaining acceptance item and the only thing left to prove before I stage and commit.
